title: cascarilla oblongifolia
intro: Cascarilla oblongifolia is a small, evergreen tree native to Central and South America, also known as the 'Bark of the White Cedar'. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of cascarilla oblongifolia include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever. The herbal preparations of cascarilla oblongifolia can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
